name: Python Covert-Tactics Scanner
description: Detect covert/evasive tactics in Python: hidden code execution (exec/eval/compile of base64/hex/zlib/marshal output, pickle deserialization, dynamic import, chr()/\x string building), environment-keyed evasion (platform/VM/sandbox/analyst detection, timezone/locale, debugger/tracer checks), and Unicode steganography. Emits STEGO/OBF/EVADE atoms and flags family co-occurrence. Static and read-only.

prerequisites.install_hint: Install Python 3.8+ — pure stdlib, no other dependency.

safety.notes: Read-only static scan (regex + Unicode analysis). Never imports, compiles, or executes the input.

args.name: input
args.desc: file OR directory of .py to scan

payload.note: Zero-dependency detector, the Python sibling of js-covert-scan (same STEGO/OBF/EVADE atom model). Strongest signal is decode-then-exec co-occurring with a sandbox/platform check (evasive dropper). Pairs with pyc-decompile for the Python-malware path.
